blockingIssues.description: Carried forward from round 1: `pyproject.toml` still specifies `pybaseball @ git+https://github.com/jldbc/pybaseball.git` instead of the feature's explicit `pybaseball>=2.3` requirement, and the current virtualenv still reports pybaseball 2.2.7. No dedicated fix feature has landed for this mismatch yet.

`fetch_fielding_stats()` still returns an existing `fielding_{season}.parquet` unchanged on the default `refresh=False` path, so qualified-only parquet data written by the original failed feature remains in use unless callers manually delete or refresh the cache.

`fetch_catcher_framing()` has the same upgrade-path bug for existing `catcher_framing_{season}.parquet` files, leaving previously persisted qualified-only framing data in place unless callers force a refetch or remove the cache.

appliedUpdates.description: Added the missing `worker-ba06611d isolate unrelated data_builder WIP` stash note to `.factory/library/workspace-state.md` so future workers can account for the repo-local stash surfaced during lineup-fix validation.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the Windows-specific initialization and validation fallback (direct `.venv\Scripts\python.exe` commands and the existing `*_windows` manifest commands) instead of assuming `.factory/init.sh`, bare `pytest`, and glob-based `py_compile` work on Windows.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Fix reviews for fix-t5-odds-api-commit-games, fix-t6-pybaseball-raw-fielding, fix-t7-lineup-date-scoping, and fix-t8-weather-cache-ttl all reported justified deviations because `.factory/init.sh` or the generic shell/glob commands were not reliable in this Windows repo, even though the workaround is already documented in `.factory/library/environment.md` and `.factory/services.yaml`.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Promote Ruff to the canonical lint command (or rename the current `lint` entry to compile) so the manifest's primary lint label matches how scrutiny validators actually run linting.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The fix-t5-odds-api-commit-games review found that `.factory/services.yaml` still defines `lint` as `py_compile` while actual scrutiny lint validation uses `ruff check`, so the current canonical lint label is misleading for future workers.

rejectedObservations.observation: Repeated Windows startup/validation mismatch notes across the four fix reviews.
rejectedObservations.reason: deduplicated into one systemic data-worker skill recommendation rather than listing the same process gap four times.
